summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that her book 'Weapons of Math Destruction' is entering the pre-publication phase, with blurbs being solicited from carefully selected readers. She reflects on the psychology of this moment—enjoying positive early feedback while bracing for inevitable criticism when the book reaches a wider audience. She also announces a book tour starting in September 2016, primarily on the East Coast with possible stops in San Francisco and the Midwest, and solicits suggestions from readers for venues and communities to visit.
key_points: The book is in the 'blurb phase' where publishers send copies to influential people likely to respond positively
O'Neil acknowledges the book was written to be provocative and anticipates critical backlash
She's savoring the brief window of mostly positive feedback before wider public scrutiny
The book tour will begin September 6th, 2016, ideally kicking off with a party featuring her band
money_quotes: We've carefully chosen people who probably won't hate the book.
I'd like to hold on to this moment for as long as possible, because when the book is reviewed more widely, the critics – at least some of them – will hate the book.
That will be tough, but of course I wrote it to be provocative.
So I hope my skin is thick enough for that.
key_insight: The pre-publication period offers authors a brief, curated window of positive feedback before facing the full spectrum of public criticism that provocative work inevitably attracts.
